title: Payroll and Tax Calculations
description: Build a complete payroll spreadsheet that calculates gross pay, allowances, deductions, and net pay for employees.
objectives: Calculate basic pay from hours worked and rate per hour
Calculate allowances as percentages of basic pay
Calculate gross pay, tax deductions, and net pay
content:
Payroll Is Essential

Every organization must pay its employees accurately and on time. Payroll calculations involve hours worked, hourly rates, allowances, tax deductions, and net pay. A single error in payroll can cause employee dissatisfaction, legal issues, and financial loss. Excel is the standard tool for preparing payroll in small and medium businesses.

Payroll Structure

A typical payroll includes these elements:

  • Basic Pay: Hours worked × Pay per hour
  • Allowances: Housing, transport, or other benefits (often a percentage of basic pay)
  • Gross Pay: Basic Pay + Allowances
  • Deductions: Tax, NHIF, NSSF, or other mandatory deductions
  • Net Pay: Gross Pay − Deductions

Sample Payroll Table

NameHours WorkedPay/HourBasic PayAllowance (15%)Gross PayTax (20%)Net Pay
Korir F.121,50018,0002,70020,7004,14016,560
Atieno J.2865018,2002,73020,9304,18616,744

Formulas Used

  • Basic Pay: =B2*C2 (Hours × Rate)
  • Allowance: =D2*0.15 (15% of Basic Pay)
  • Gross Pay: =D2+E2 (Basic + Allowance)
  • Tax: =F2*0.20 (20% of Gross Pay)
  • Net Pay: =F2-G2 (Gross − Tax)

Workplace Scenario

You are the payroll assistant at Bata Shoe Company. The personnel manager assigns you the task of compiling the monthly payroll for 10 employees. You enter hours worked and pay per hour for each employee. Excel calculates basic pay, housing allowance (15% of basic), transport allowance (a fixed amount), gross pay, NHIF deduction (fixed), NSSF deduction (fixed), tax (20% of gross), and finally net pay. You format all figures as currency (Ksh) and print the payroll for the finance manager to review.
exercise: Create a payroll spreadsheet for 5 employees with these columns: Name, Hours Worked, Pay Per Hour, Basic Pay, Allowance (15% of Basic), Gross Pay, Tax (20% of Gross), Net Pay. Use formulas for all calculated columns. Format all money values as currency. Add a row at the bottom with TOTAL for each column using SUM. Save as Payroll_Practice.xlsx.

quiz.question: What is the formula for Basic Pay?
quiz.explanation: Basic Pay is calculated by multiplying the hours worked by the pay per hour.

quiz.question: If an employee's Gross Pay is 20,000 and tax is 20%, what is the tax amount?
What does Net Pay represent?
quiz.explanation: Net Pay is the amount the employee actually receives after all deductions (tax, NHIF, NSSF) are subtracted from Gross Pay.

quiz.question: Why should you format payroll figures as currency?
options: To show the currency symbol and two decimal places for clarity and professionalism
quiz.explanation: Currency format displays values with a symbol and two decimal places, which is standard for financial documents and prevents misreading.
